title: Masking My Autism Made Me Sick
description:
My Missing Words by Sarah Jane Cody “I feel too much,” I confessed to Noel early on in our relationship. I had no other words for it, but it felt important, like maybe I should try to warn him. Shortly after we began dating, I lay down on the ground outside a coffee shop unable […]

The post Masking My Autism Made Me Sick appeared first on Electric Literature.<
hook: A deeply personal essay on masking autism and its physical toll—complex interiority meets embodied experience.

title: Tyrant Style
description: “This was proper writing, pretentious even. Style pervaded right down to the sentence, as if every piece was assembled from the best sentences its writer had or would ever come up with.”
hook: Paris Review explores how style becomes tyranny in writing—literary criticism that reveals craft obsession and creative ambition.

title: Victor Frankenstein, ABD
description:
What happens to a young scientist whose primary mentor is an artificial intelligence?

The post Victor Frankenstein, ABD appeared first on Public Books.
hook: What happens when AI mentors young scientists? A philosophical thought experiment on knowledge, creation, and intellectual formation.

title: The Dark and Stormy Tale of a ShantyTok Band
description: When a scraggly band of folk musicians arrived to tour the UK, residents of a small Welsh town were enamored—until they learned that the band’s leader ruled with an iron fi
hook: Deep dive into ShantyTok fandom—obsessive cultural analysis of how niche internet communities reshape music and identity.

title: On her own terms
description:
Doris Lessing’s Golden Notebook remains shocking, necessary and imperfect – a dazzling experiment in living as a woman

- by Catherine Taylor
hook: Doris Lessing on her own terms—late-bloomer reinvention and female interiority examined through a literary icon's life.
